response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and systems developer with a broad polyglot background, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The job demands a C++ specialist with 8–12 years of C++ experience in industrial/IoT contexts, adherence to IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle, and familiarity with industrial protocols like OPC UA, Modbus, and MQTT. His C++ exposure is limited to a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; his primary expertise lies in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web and data platforms. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, embedded protocol experience, or security-standards compliance work, making him a poor fit despite his general engineering caliber.
strengths: Demonstrated experience building high-performance, high-throughput systems (millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic) shows an understanding of low-latency and concurrency concerns
Background in event-driven and distributed architectures (GISCollective, microservices at Sociomantic) is conceptually adjacent to message-broker and IoT patterns
Long career with consistent seniority progression and strong community engagement (workshops, talks, open-source) signals a high-quality engineering mindset
concerns: C++ proficiency is effectively absent from his modern career — the only directly relevant experience is a six-month junior role in 2014–2015 and a thesis project, both over a decade old
Zero exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, or any of the required industrial protocols (OPC UA, Modbus TCP/IP, MQTT, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
Education (B.S. Computer Science + Fine Arts) does not meet the B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech engineering-degree requirement typical for this industry segment
